_notes: Selector type: email. Covers (1) account existence across sites, (2) breach/credential exposure, (3) deliverability/verification, (4) email->identity. holehe + emailrep already wired in src/tools/email_tools.py. emailrep.io free tier is effectively dead (folded into Sublime Security) — flagged. HIBP is the only authoritative breach source and needs a ~$3.95/mo key; Hudson Rock Cavalier gives free infostealer-exposure coverage as the no-cost breach pick.

best.name: holehe
best.maintained: stale (last code push 2024-09-10, v1.61; NOT archived, 11.3k stars, 76 open issues). Still the best free email->account-existence CLI (~120 sites).
best.notes: Already wired as HoleheTool. Uses password-reset / account-existence probes, does not alert the target. Expect silent module breakage + rate-limit false-negatives because of staleness — isolate and treat negatives as low-confidence. Per-module results; add --only-used to cut noise. Has -C/--csv and -j/--json options for structured output if the wrapper is upgraded (current wrapper parses stdout).

runner_up.name: GHunt
runner_up.notes: Deep Gmail/Google-account existence + linked-services pivot (real name, Maps reviews, YouTube channel, profile photo). Needs valid Google account cookies via 'ghunt login' -> STATEFUL, not a clean stateless collector. Requires credential vault + auth-expiry handling. Best treated as email->identity, not generic existence. Listed as runner-up because of the cookie/auth burden.

capabilities.gaps: holehe is the only maintained free generic email->account CLI and it is stale; no actively-maintained free drop-in replacement exists. The 'User-Scanner replaces holehe' claim is an unbenchmarked promo post — do not adopt on faith. Many richer existence/identity sources (Epieos, OSINT Industries, Castrick) are web-only or paid (see excluded).

best.name: Have I Been Pwned API v3 (breachedaccount)
best.maintained: active (Troy Hunt; v3 stable, current). Most-cited breach source.
best.notes: PAID key required (~$3.50-$3.95/mo Pwned 1 tier) for any email-search endpoint; key is a 32-char hex string sent in the hibp-api-key header, user-agent header also mandatory. Returns JSON array of breach objects (Name, BreachDate, DataClasses, IsVerified...). 404 = no breaches (clean), 200 = breached. Rate-limited per subscription tier (handle 429 with backoff). Companion endpoints on same key: /pasteaccount/{email}, /stealerlogsbyemail/{email}. Authoritative, low false-positive — make this the primary breach collector. Outputs breach names/data-classes, not new pivotable selectors.

best.name: Hudson Rock Cavalier - search by email (free OSINT endpoint)
best.maintained: active (Hudson Rock; free OSINT tier of the commercial Cavalier infostealer DB).
best.notes: FREE, no key on the public osint-tools/search-by-email endpoint -> best free breach/exposure pick. Returns infostealer-infection exposure: whether the email appears as a victim and/or in stolen credential logs, plus stealer family, antivirus, date_compromised, and (partially redacted) lists of websites/credentials exposed. Complements HIBP (HIBP = public breach corpora; Hudson Rock = infostealer/stealer-log exposure). NOTE: the authenticated commercial API (docs.hudsonrock.com /search-by-login/emails, POST, needs key) is a different, paid endpoint — wire the free GET osint-tools one. Free monitoring tier covers up to 3 emails/3 domains/3 usernames; expect 429 backoff on the public endpoint.

runner_up.name: HIBP Pwned Passwords (k-anonymity)
runner_up.notes: Free, keyless, k-anonymity password-hash range check. Not email-keyed (needs a candidate password), so not a primary email-breach collector — keep only if a credential pair is in scope.

capabilities.gaps: Authoritative email-keyed breach search (HIBP) is paid. Free breach coverage is limited to infostealer exposure (Hudson Rock) which misses classic credential-dump breaches. DeHashed/LeakCheck/IntelX would add raw leaked-record depth but are paid (IntelX has a ~50/day free tier — see excluded). No free tool returns the actual plaintext leaked password/record without a paid subscription.

best.name: Disify
best.notes: FREE, NO key, no signup -> best deliverability pick. One call returns JSON {format, dns, whitelist, disposable} validating syntax + MX/DNS presence + disposable/temporary-domain flag. No live SMTP mailbox probe (so it confirms domain-deliverable, not mailbox-exists), but it is keyless and unlimited-enough for automation. Yields a quality/triage signal (real vs disposable vs invalid), not new selectors.

runner_up.name: MailTester / Reacher-style SMTP probe
runner_up.notes: For true mailbox-level SMTP deliverability (catch-all/role/SMTP-accept) use a keyed verifier (Abstract API / EmailVerifierApi / Maileroo / Gamalogic) — all have small free tiers (100-500 credits) but require signup. Wire only if mailbox-exists certainty (beyond Disify's domain check) is needed. Self-hosting reacherhq/check-if-email-exists (Rust/CLI) is the keyless alternative but is heavyweight and gets blocked by major providers.

capabilities.gaps: Keyless free APIs (Disify) only do syntax+MX+disposable, not live SMTP mailbox verification. True mailbox-exists checks need a keyed verifier (free tier w/ signup) or a self-hosted SMTP prober that major mailbox providers (Gmail/Outlook) actively block.

best.name: GHunt (email -> Google identity)
best.notes: Best free email->real-identity pivot for Gmail/Google-hosted emails: returns Google account display name, profile photo, Maps reviews, YouTube channel, GAIA ID, linked services. STATEFUL — needs valid Google cookies loaded via 'ghunt login' (listener/base64/manual); cookies expire -> needs a credential vault + re-auth alerting, hence exec_category=custom. Only works for emails tied to a Google account.

best.name: Gravatar (email hash lookup)
best.notes: FREE, keyless. MD5-hash the lowercased/trimmed email and GET the .json profile. When present returns display name, username, linked accounts, URLs, and avatar -> direct email->name/username pivot. Already implemented for the 'name' selector as gravatar_check (src/tools/name_tools.py) but NOT wired for the email selector — small wrapper add. Sparse coverage (only emails with a Gravatar) but zero-cost and high-precision when it hits.

runner_up.name: holehe (account existence -> username pivot)
runner_up.notes: Existence hits across ~120 sites indirectly attribute identity (which platforms the person uses); some modules leak partial usernames/recovery hints. Secondary to GHunt/Gravatar for actual name attribution.

capabilities.gaps: Free email->real-name attribution is essentially limited to Gmail (GHunt, cookie-gated) and emails with a Gravatar. General-purpose email->identity (any provider) is paid-data territory: OSINT Industries, Epieos, Pipl, Predicta, DeHashed (all excluded below — web-only or paid). No maintained free tool closes the last mile to identity for arbitrary email domains.

excluded.reason: Already wired (EmailRepTool) but free tier folded into Sublime Security; emailrep.io public endpoint is effectively dead / heavily rate-limited without a Sublime key. Keep wrapper but expect failures; do not rely on it.
Abandoned (audit-flagged dead). Superseded by HIBP + Hudson Rock.
Abandoned (audit-flagged dead).

Not single-email scoped — it is domain/company-scoped (harvests emails/subdomains/hosts for an org). Actively maintained (laramies, ~v4.9+, releases through 2025-26) and worth wiring under a DOMAIN/company bucket, but out of scope for an email selector.
Paid only (no usable free automation tier). Strong leaked-record depth; revisit if budget allows. Web/API but key-gated.
Paid/keyed leak search; no free automation tier.
Has a ~50-lookup/day free tier (could be promoted later) but free tier is heavily limited and ToS-restricted for automation; defer.
Paid commercial email/phone enrichment (web + paid API). Best-in-class last-mile identity but no free tier.
Web-interface only for the free tier (manual); strong email->Google/identity but not a structured collector. Paid API exists.
Paid identity-resolution API, enterprise-gated.
Enterprise paid breach/credential exposure; no free automation.
Email finder/verifier for ORG recon (domain-scoped), keyed; belongs in a domain/company bucket, not single-email. Has small free tier.
Keyed email-verification API; redundant with keyless Disify for our needs (use Disify free; mailboxlayer only if SMTP-level + key acceptable).
Dead/abandoned email-verification service (public API discontinued).
Web-interface only, manual; no structured API.
Web-interface only, manual.
Pastebin-dump search, web-interface; API exists but narrow/unreliable — defer to HIBP paste endpoint.
Shut down 2026-02-07 (audit-flagged dead).
OSS abandoned after Intel 471 acquisition (2+ yrs stale); not used as orchestration.
GUI framework / paid transforms — not a structured headless collector.
Framework/orchestration, not a single-capability collector; most modules need their own keys.
